title: A2A Protocol Messages (DEPRECATED)
x-deprecation-notice: DEPRECATED as of 2026-04-04. Superseded by single-orchestrator + tool-bundle architecture. See contracts/ga-orchestrator-architecture.md, schemas/blackboard-state.schema.json, and contracts/ga-mcp-tool-bundles.md. The hub-and-spoke coordinator pattern defined here was rejected after adversarial debate (2-4x more hops than necessary, coordinator SPOF, aggregation ambiguity). Retained for reference and possible future multi-agent scenarios (e.g., cross-organizational federation) where true agent-to-agent messaging is required.
description: [DEPRECATED — see x-deprecation-notice] Agent-to-Agent protocol message schemas for the GuitarAlchemist music AI ecosystem. Defines agent messages, routing requests, aggregation responses, error responses, and MCP tool invocations.

confidence.description: Calibrated confidence score. >= 0.9 autonomous, >= 0.7 proceed with note, >= 0.5 confirm, >= 0.3 escalate, < 0.3 do not act.

hexavalent_truth.description: Hexavalent truth value: True, Probable, Unknown, Doubtful, False, Contradictory.

agent_id.description: Kebab-case agent identifier matching a persona name.

message_integrity.description: Galactic Protocol integrity fields for tamper detection and replay prevention.

message_id.description: UUID v4 for deduplication and audit trail.

origin_agent.description: Persona name of the sending agent.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the message payload (excluding integrity fields).

agent_message.title: Agent Message
agent_message.description: Core message format for agent-to-agent communication within the music AI ecosystem.

sender.description: Persona name of the sending agent.

receiver.description: Persona name of the intended recipient agent.

intent.description: The purpose of this message. query: requesting information. response: answering a query. delegate: forwarding to a more appropriate agent. notify: informational broadcast. escalate: raising to higher authority. teach: Seldon knowledge transfer. assess: comprehension verification. tool-invoke: requesting MCP tool execution. tool-result: returning MCP tool output.

payload.description: Message content. Structure varies by intent.

content.description: Primary text content of the message.

structured_data.description: Machine-readable structured payload (chord analysis results, scale data, tab notation, etc.).

context.description: Contextual metadata carried with the message.

conversation_id.description: Groups related messages into a conversation thread.

parent_message_id.description: References the message this is responding to.

domain_context.description: Domain-specific context (key, tempo, tuning, skill level, etc.).

confidence.description: Sender's calibrated confidence in the payload content.

truth_value.description: Hexavalent truth assessment of the payload content.

channel.description: Crisp (machine-actionable) or fuzzy (explanatory for humans). Per Galactic Protocol channel semantics.

routing_request.title: Routing Request
routing_request.description: Request to the orchestrator to route a query to the most appropriate agent(s). Uses affordance matching per Galactic Protocol.

query.description: The user or agent query to be routed.

domain_hints.description: Hints about which domains are relevant (e.g., 'theory', 'technique', 'composition').

required_capabilities.description: Explicit capability requirements for affordance matching.

skill_level.description: Learner skill level for pedagogical routing.

prior_agents.description: Agents already consulted in this conversation (prevents loops).

preferred_agent.description: Explicitly preferred agent. If specified, skips affordance matching and routes directly (per Galactic Protocol fallback rules).

fallback_chain.description: Ordered list of fallback agents if the preferred or matched agent is unavailable or declines.

routing_strategy.description: single: route to one agent. fan-out: send to multiple agents in parallel, aggregate. chain: sequential pipeline through agents.

max_agents.description: Maximum number of agents to involve (for fan-out strategy).

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for agent responses in milliseconds.

aggregation_response.title: Aggregation Response
aggregation_response.description: Aggregated response from multiple agents, combining primary and supporting responses with confidence scoring and source attribution.

primary_response.description: The highest-confidence or most relevant agent response selected as primary.

relationship.description: How this supporting response relates to the primary.

supporting_responses.description: Additional responses from other agents that supplement the primary.

confidence_scores.description: Confidence metrics across all participating agents.

aggregate.description: Weighted aggregate confidence across all responses.

agreement_level.description: Level of agreement among responding agents.

per_agent.description: Individual confidence scores keyed by agent_id.

source_type.description: How the agent produced its response.

references.description: Specific references (theory rules, tab sources, course materials).

routing_metadata.description: Metadata about how the routing was performed.

total_duration_ms.description: Total time from routing request to aggregated response.

error_response.title: Error Response
error_response.description: Standardized error response when an agent cannot fulfill a request.

error_type.description: Categorized error type for programmatic handling.

agent_id.description: The agent that encountered the error.

confidence_at_failure.description: The confidence level when the agent decided it could not proceed.

constitutional_article.description: If constitutional_violation: which article was at risk.

attempted_action.description: What the agent was trying to do when the error occurred.

suggested_agent.description: An alternative agent that may be able to handle the request.

modified_query.description: A reformulated query that might succeed.

escalation_target.description: Who to escalate to if no agent can handle it.

mcp_tool_invocation.title: MCP Tool Invocation
mcp_tool_invocation.description: Request from one agent to invoke an MCP tool, either directly or via another agent that has the tool affordance.

parameters.description: Tool-specific parameters. Validated against the tool's own parameter schema.

agent_requesting.description: The agent requesting the tool invocation.

agent_executing.description: The agent that owns the tool affordance and will execute. If omitted, the orchestrator routes by tool-agent mapping.

result_format.description: Expected format of the tool result.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for tool execution.

conversation_id.description: Links this tool invocation to its conversation thread.

mcp_tool_result.title: MCP Tool Result
mcp_tool_result.description: Result of an MCP tool invocation, returned from the executing agent to the requesting agent.

result.description: Tool output. Structure depends on the tool.

data.description: Primary result data (type varies by tool).

payload.content: The ii-V-I in C major is Dm7-G7-Cmaj7. For reharmonization, consider tritone substitution on the V: Dm7-Db7-Cmaj7.

examples.query: How do I play a Cmaj7 chord and what makes it sound jazzy?

examples.message: Cannot assess fingering feasibility for 10-string extended range guitar — outside training domain.
